briefings.headline: Regulators Publish Updated List of Distressed and Underserved Nonmetropolitan Middle-Income Areas
briefings.body: Federal agencies have released an updated list identifying distressed or underserved nonmetropolitan middle-income geographies. These designations influence how financial institutions receive credit under community reinvestment frameworks for lending and investment activity in qualifying areas. Communities on the list could see increased bank interest in local lending, small business financing, and community development projects as institutions seek to meet regulatory obligations.

sources.title: Agencies release list of distressed or underserved nonmetropolitan middle-income geographies
The Sewer Knows What You Ate

briefings.why_it_matters: Residents and small businesses in designated rural and small-town areas could find it easier to access loans and investment capital as banks direct more resources toward these communities to satisfy federal requirements.

briefings.headline: Federal Agencies Issue Joint Guidance on Protecting Sensitive Data During Bank Exams
briefings.body: Federal banking agencies have released a joint statement addressing how highly sensitive information should be handled during bank examinations. The guidance aims to establish clearer standards for managing confidential data collected in the examination process, reflecting ongoing concerns about information security within the regulatory framework that governs financial institutions.

sources.title: Agencies issue joint statement on handling of highly sensitive information during bank examinations
Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former chief lending officer of Heritage State Bank

briefings.why_it_matters: Stronger handling standards for sensitive bank examination data could reduce the risk of confidential financial information being misused or improperly disclosed, offering greater protection to consumers and institutions alike.

briefings.headline: Trump Administration Set to Announce Saudi Nuclear Fuel Deal
briefings.body: The Trump administration is preparing to announce a deal that could allow Saudi Arabia to enrich nuclear fuel. U.S. lawmakers and Israeli officials have voiced opposition to the plan, warning that a civilian nuclear program could provide Saudi Arabia a pathway to develop nuclear weapons. The announcement was expected Wednesday.

sources.title: Trump Administration to Announce Deal That Could Let Saudis Enrich Nuclear Fuel

briefings.why_it_matters: If Saudi Arabia gains uranium enrichment capability, it could shift the nuclear balance in the Middle East, potentially prompting other regional powers to pursue similar programs and raising proliferation risks that affect global security.

briefings.headline: Scientists Discover First Known Contagious Cancer Spreading Among Fish in a Lake
briefings.body: Researchers have identified what appears to be the first transmissible cancer ever documented in fish, adding a new species to the rare list of animals known to harbor contagious tumors. Previously, such cancers had been observed in only a handful of species. The discovery raises scientific questions about how widely transmissible cancers could exist in nature, and whether similar mechanisms could theoretically occur in humans.

sources.title: Deep in This Lake, a Contagious Cancer Is Spreading

briefings.why_it_matters: Expanding our understanding of how cancers can spread between animals could reshape research into cancer transmission risks and inform future public health precautions.

briefings.headline: Political appointee review of federal grants could narrow education research, groups warn
briefings.body: A proposed 100-page overhaul of federal grant rules would add a new review layer by political appointees before research funding is approved. Education research organizations say the change could make it harder to produce the kind of wide-ranging, multi-decade work that built the science of reading movement, which drew on cognitive science, neuroscience, psychology, linguistics, and classroom studies.

sources.title: Education researchers warn political review of federal grants could reshape what gets studied

briefings.why_it_matters: If political review shapes which education studies get funded, the evidence base used to design reading programs, school policies, and classroom practices could narrow or shift, affecting how children across the country are taught.

briefings.headline: TreeSize shifts business model, requiring subscription to maintain perpetual-license support
briefings.body: TreeSize is changing how it handles support for perpetual-license holders, requiring users to subscribe in order to continue receiving support. The company cited current economic conditions as the driver behind the policy shift. Users who purchased a one-time license may now face the choice of paying ongoing subscription fees or losing access to technical support for software they already own.

sources.title: TreeSize won't renew perpetual-license support unless users subscribe

briefings.why_it_matters: Perpetual-license holders who expected lifetime ownership without recurring costs could find themselves locked into subscription payments just to keep their software supported.

quick_hits.text: Cameron Hamilton, who once defended FEMA against elimination, is poised to be confirmed as the agency's administrator for a second time.

source.title: Cameron Hamilton Poised to Lead FEMA Once More

quick_hits.text: The Federal Reserve released initial findings from its 2025 triennial payments study, tracking how Americans use checks, cards, and digital transfers.

source.title: Federal Reserve issues initial findings from its 2025 triennial payments study

quick_hits.text: A study found 1,700 HIV treatment sites closed following Trump administration foreign aid cuts, disproportionately affecting children and high-risk adults.

source.title: 1,700 H.I.V. Treatment Sites Closed After Trump Aid Cuts, a Study Finds

quick_hits.text: A randomized trial found students whose teachers used AI tools for lesson planning and feedback scored lower on learning outcomes than peers in traditional classrooms.

source.title: Teachers save time with AI. Their students may pay the price

quick_hits.text: Steam Deck sales fell 80 percent after Valve raised prices, according to analysis of recent bestseller charts.

source.title: Sales of Valve's Steam Deck crater after recent price hike

voice_blocks.title: This Day
voice_blocks.text: 2019: Chandrayaan-2, the second lunar exploration mission developed by Indian Space Research Organisation after Chandrayaan-1 is launched from Satish Dhawan Space Centre in a GSLV Mark III M1. It consists of a lunar orbiter, and also included the Vikram lander, and the Pragyan lunar rover.

closer.text: "Knowledge will forever govern ignorance; and a people who mean to be their own governors must arm themselves with the power which knowledge gives."
closer.attribution: James Madison, letter to W.T. Barry (1822)

source.title: James Madison, letter to W.T. Barry (1822)
